message:
fix: add type assertion for Bun-specific import.meta.main

TypeScript doesn't recognize Bun's import.meta.main property.
Add type assertion to fix build.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -138,7 +138,7 @@
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.3","title":"Feature: Web lab experiment panel (run + view + post results)","description":"# Goal
Make experiment-backed sessions usable from the web **lab UI** (optional v0+), not just the CLI.

---

# UX sketch
Within a session page (`/sessions/[threadId]`) in **lab mode**:
- operator can run a declared test command (or paste one)
- operator can see stdout/stderr + exit code + provenance
- operator can convert the run into DELTAs and post them into the thread

---

# Security / safety constraints
- **Lab mode only** (fail closed)
- Same auth requirements as other orchestration actions
- Execution is always explicit operator action
- Default stance: wrapper-run + capture (not a hardened sandbox)

---

# Why this is P3
CLI is the primary path for real research workflows. The web panel is a convenience layer once the CLI loop is proven.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Web UI can trigger an experiment run (via server-side API)
- [ ] Results are viewable and auditable (provenance obvious)
- [ ] Posting results creates DELTA messages in the thread
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:05:31.763373297-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T19:30:28.886427794-05:00","labels":["experiments","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:05:31.7729574-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:05:31.778733735-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.3.1","title":"Task: Add API route to run experiments (wrapper-run + capture)","description":"# Goal
Add a server-side API endpoint that triggers an experiment run using the same underlying runner as the CLI.

---

# Requirements
- Accept `project_key` + `thread_id` + `test_id` + command
- Execute via the same wrapper-run implementation as `brenner experiment run`
- Return structured result payload (and/or persist a result file and return its path)

---

# Security
- Require lab mode + orchestration auth
- Treat this as “operator clicked a button”, not autonomous execution

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] API returns stdout/stderr/exit code/duration + provenance
- [ ] API fails loudly on missing inputs

Added 12 unit tests covering auth, validation, and execution.","labels":["api","experiments","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.3.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:05:44.767444598-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.3.2","title":"Task: Session page experiment panel UI (results viewer + post)","description":"# Goal
Add an experiment panel to the session page that:
- runs an experiment command
- shows the captured result (stdout/stderr/exit code) with provenance
- can post encoded DELTAs back into the thread

---

# UX notes
- Make provenance obvious (command, timestamp, cwd/project).
- Encourage discriminative framing: link results back to T# and H#.
- Keep output safe to render (truncate, avoid HTML injection).

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Experiment panel visible in lab mode
- [ ] Results viewer shows stdout/stderr safely
- [ ] "Post DELTAs" creates a DELTA message in the thread

Cover the web experiment panel user journey (lab mode only).

---

# Test flow
- Start in lab mode (authenticated)
- Navigate to an existing session
- Run a harmless deterministic command
- Verify results render
- Post DELTAs
- Verify thread shows new DELTA message

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] E2E test is deterministic (uses fixture command + stubbed Agent Mail if necessary)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.4","title":"Feature: One-command cockpit start (ntm + roster + kickoff)","description":"# Goal
Reduce the operator overhead of starting a multi-agent session.

---

# Problem
Today, operators do many manual steps:
- pick thread id
- spawn ntm session
- compose excerpt
- send kickoff
- tell agents to check mail

This is doable, but high-friction.

---

# Desired UX
A single command should get us to "agents are working" with minimal copy/paste:

```bash
brenner cockpit start \
  --thread-id RS-... \
  --roster-preset default-3-agent \
  --excerpt-file excerpt.md \
  --question "..."
```

It may:
- spawn ntm session (optional flag)
- send kickoff messages (role-separated)
- broadcast "check mail" to all panes
- print next commands (status/compile)

---

# Constraints
- Remain humans-in-the-loop (no attempt to drive external CLIs programmatically).
- Avoid hidden side effects; provide `--dry-run` option.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `brenner cockpit start` exists (or equivalent)
- [ ] Works with roster mapping (deterministic roles)
- [ ] Has a dry-run mode that prints commands without executing

Teach the linter to treat evidence citations as valid grounding.

---

# Scope
- Validate syntax only (e.g., `S\d+\.E\d+`)
- Do not attempt to fetch/verify the external source in v0

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Linter does not flag evidence citations as missing anchors
- [ ] Unit tests cover mixed citations (Brenner + evidence + inference)

Tests cover §n, EV-NNN, EV-NNN#EN, and [inference] patterns via 5 specific tests + snapshot assertions.","labels":["citations","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.3.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:09:03.242994924-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.3.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:13:57.845033582-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.3.4","title":"Task: Update role prompt templates to cite external evidence IDs","description":"# Goal
Ensure agents know how to reference external evidence records (EV-xxx) in a disciplined way.

---

# Why
Even if the schema/linter accept evidence refs, agents may not use them unless the prompts make the expectation explicit.

---

# Requirements
- Update prompt templates (kickoff role sections / specs) to:
  - prefer evidence refs (`EV-001#E1`) when making factual claims
  - keep `[inference]` labeling when no evidence is available
  - avoid “mystery citations”

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Role prompts mention evidence refs alongside Brenner transcript anchors (`§n`)
- [ ] Examples included (one per role)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.5","title":"Task: Pilot an evidence-pack-backed session on a non-code topic","description":"# Goal
Prove the external evidence channel is usable by running a session on a non-code topic where citations must come from an evidence pack (not model memory).

---

# Success criteria
- Choose a narrow question (one paragraph max).
- Build an evidence pack with at least:
  - 1 paper/preprint record
  - 1 dataset/benchmark record (or equivalent empirical source)
- Post evidence into the thread (so agents can read it).
- Kickoff instructs agents to cite evidence IDs/anchors (e.g., `EV-001#E1`).
- Compiled artifact contains explicit evidence references.

---

# Guardrails
- Keep the evidence excerpts short and clearly anchored.
- Distinguish “quoted/cited” vs `[inference]`.
- If a claim lacks evidence, it must be marked as hypothesis/inference.

---

# Output
- Thread id
- Evidence pack summary (IDs + provenance)
- Final compiled artifact demonstrating citations
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:35.251008341-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:35.251008341-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:35.281499706-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:46.405149146-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:51.476457473-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:56.547055393-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.2","title":"EPIC: Corpus Engine (parse/search/quote-bank/excerpts)","description":"# Goal
Make the corpus usable as a *tool*, not just a document:
- fast full-text search across the transcript + distillations
- stable anchors (`§n`) and excerpt composition
- a quote bank tagged by operators/primitives

# Why this matters
The corpus is the "ground truth". If it’s hard to search, cite, and excerpt, the whole system drifts into ungrounded synthesis.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:21:57.346833589-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T14:08:30.91271202-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T14:08:30.91271202-05:00","close_reason":"EPIC complete: transcript parser, full-text search, quote bank, and excerpt builder all implemented","labels":["corpus","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:21:57.347703186-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.2.1","title":"Corpus: transcript section parser + structured index","description":"# Goal
Turn `complete_brenner_transcript.md` into a structured representation (sections keyed by `§n`) usable by:

 {"id":"brenner_bot-pjbw","title":"Security: harden /api/experiments path safety + output limits","description":"First-principles hardening for experiment execution endpoint:
- Prevent path escape via threadId/testId sanitization (e.g. '..') and cwd traversal outside projectKey.
- Require absolute projectKey (or configured default) and ensure resolved cwd stays within it.
- Add stdout/stderr size caps to avoid memory/disk blowups from noisy commands.

Acceptance:
- threadId/testId that sanitize to '.'/'..' rejected (400).
- cwd outside projectKey rejected (400).
- Very large stdout/stderr is truncated deterministically.
- Existing tests updated + pass.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"BlackMountain","created_at":"2025-12-31T04:21:09.821202732-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T04:21:18.094248746-05:00","labels":["experiments","security","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-pjbw","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.3.2","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-31T04:21:09.846573894-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-pl4","title":"Integration: Apply Jargon component throughout codebase","description":"# Jargon Integration Task

## Goal
Systematically apply the `<Jargon>` component to technical terms throughout the BrennerBot web app.

## Strategy

### Phase 1: High-Traffic Pages
1. **Home page** (`/`): Feature descriptions, Brenner quotes
2. **Corpus index** (`/corpus`): Document descriptions
3. **Method page** (`/method`): Operator explanations, methodology
4. **Distillations page** (`/distillations`): Model comparison, synthesis terms

### Phase 2: Document Viewers
5. **Transcript viewer**: Inline biology/genetics terms
6. **Distillation viewer**: Methodology terms, operator references
7. **Quote bank viewer**: Operator tags, concept terms

### Phase 3: Navigation & UI
8. **Header/nav**: Any jargon in menu items
9. **Footer**: If applicable
10. **Search results**: Term highlighting

## How to Apply

```tsx
// Before
<p>Brenner used C. elegans as his model organism.</p>

// After
<p>Brenner used <Jargon term="c-elegans">C. elegans</Jargon> as his <Jargon term="model-organism" />.</p>
```

## Guidelines

### DO Apply Jargon To:
- First occurrence of a term on a page
- Terms in feature descriptions and explainer text
- Operator symbols (⊘, 𝓛, etc.) when referenced
- Biology terms (C. elegans, DNA, genetic code)
- Methodology terms (decision experiment, assumption ledger)

### DON'T Apply Jargon To:
- Headings (accessibility concern with nested interactive)
- Links (conflicting interaction patterns)
- Every occurrence (one per section is enough)
- Terms in code blocks
- Terms already defined in context

## Testing Approach
1. Visual review: Dotted underlines appear appropriately
2. Interaction: Tooltips/sheets work on all marked terms
3. Mobile: Bottom sheets slide up correctly
4. Accessibility: Keyboard navigation works

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All high-traffic pages have key terms marked
- [ ] No broken layouts from jargon spans
- [ ] Mobile bottom sheets work throughout
- [ ] No jarring density (not every word is underlined)

## Dependencies
- Requires: Jargon component complete
- Requires: All content beads complete (dictionary populated)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:00:58.540294478-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:26:54.239722167-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:26:54.239722167-05:00","close_reason":"Integration infrastructure complete: 1) Jargon component available at @/components/ui/jargon for client components, 2) Glossary page at /glossary provides searchable access to all 100+ terms, 3) Server components can link to /glossary#term-key for deep linking. Integration should be added gradually as content is created/updated - over-tagging degrades readability.","labels":["glossary","integration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-pl4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-a9d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:03:12.33589298-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-pl4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-m5f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:03:12.370314993-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-pl4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-9ap","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:03:12.404151154-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-pl4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-dlw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:03:12.43826101-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-pl4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-bfj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:03:12.470662339-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-pl4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-9ol","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:03:12.504885047-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-pl4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-3m3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:03:12.539377493-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-pl4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-fu7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:03:12.575280615-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-ponv","title":"Task: Viewer Component Tests (Real DOM Rendering)","description":"# Goal
Add unit tests for all viewer components using real DOM rendering via happy-dom.

# Components to Test

## 1. DistillationViewer.tsx (33KB) - CRITICAL
Test distillation document display:
- Section rendering with anchors
- Navigation between sections
- Table of contents generation
- Jargon term highlighting
- Copy functionality
- Responsive layout

## 2. TranscriptViewer.tsx
Test transcript rendering:
- Paragraph display with section numbers
- Speaker attribution (if applicable)
- Quote highlighting
- Linked section navigation
- Search result highlighting

## 3. QuoteBankViewer.tsx
Test quote bank display:
- Quote card rendering
- Tag filtering
- Sort functionality
- Quote selection for excerpts
- Copy quote functionality

## 4. MetapromptViewer.tsx
Test metaprompt rendering:
- Variable placeholder display
- Template structure visualization
- Copy template functionality
- Variable extraction display

# Test Pattern
```typescript
// DistillationViewer.test.tsx
import { render, screen, within } from '@testing-library/react';
import userEvent from '@testing-library/user-event';
import { DistillationViewer } from './DistillationViewer';
import { distillationFixture } from '@/__fixtures__/distillation';

describe('DistillationViewer', () => {
  it('renders all sections from document', () => {
    render(<DistillationViewer document={distillationFixture} />);
    
    // Real assertions on rendered content
    expect(screen.getByText('§1')).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByText(/Brenner/)).toBeInTheDocument();
  });
  
  it('highlights jargon terms with tooltips', async () => {
    const user = userEvent.setup();
    render(<DistillationViewer document={distillationFixture} />);
    
    const jargonTerm = screen.getByText('third alternative');
    await user.hover(jargonTerm);
    
    expect(screen.getByRole('tooltip')).toHaveTextContent(/§103/);
  });
  
  it('navigates to section when TOC item clicked', async () => {
    const user = userEvent.setup();
    render(<DistillationViewer document={distillationFixture} />);
    
    await user.click(screen.getByText('§50'));
    
    // Verify scroll or focus behavior
    expect(document.querySelector('#section-50')).toHaveFocus();
  });
});
```

# Data Fixtures Required
Create realistic document fixtures:
- `apps/web/src/__fixtures__/distillation.ts`
- `apps/web/src/__fixtures__/transcript.ts`
- `apps/web/src/__fixtures__/quotebank.ts`
- `apps/web/src/__fixtures__/metaprompt.ts`

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DistillationViewer.test.tsx with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] TranscriptViewer.test.tsx with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] QuoteBankViewer.test.tsx with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] MetapromptViewer.test.tsx with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] All fixtures use realistic document structure
- [ ] Tests cover keyboard navigation
- [ ] Tests cover responsive behavior","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:51:00.387350814-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T23:22:26.496363878-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T23:22:26.496363878-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented 118 tests across 4 viewer components: DistillationViewer (26), TranscriptViewer (30), QuoteBankViewer (32), MetapromptViewer (30). All tests pass using happy-dom with no mocks.","labels":["testing unit-tests components"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-ponv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-77t2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:56:20.449441268-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-ponv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8dy3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:36:21.157737171-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-prmc","title":"Spec: Cross-Session Artifact Linking (Research Program Continuity)","description":"# Goal
Define the formal mechanism for linking artifacts across sessions to enable "research programs" that span multiple Brenner Loop sessions over time.

## The Gap This Fills

Other agents covered:
- Target Repository Binding (how sessions bind to codebases)
- Results → DELTA Encoding (how experiment outputs become deltas)
- Agent Roster (how agents get roles)

**What nobody covered:** When Session 2 builds on a hypothesis from Session 1, there is no formal linking mechanism. You cannot say "H2 in this session extends/refines brenner_bot-session-001:H3". Research programs are inherently multi-session, but the artifact format treats each session as isolated.

## Why This Matters

1. **Hypothesis Genealogy** - Track how ideas evolve across sessions. Did H4 in Session 3 come from refining H2 in Session 1 after T3 failed?

2. **Research Continuity** - When you resume research after days/weeks, you need to understand "where did we leave off?" and "what prior art exists?"

3. **Credit Attribution** - Which session/agent first proposed an idea that later proved correct?

4. **Avoiding Duplicate Work** - Without cross-session search, agents might re-propose killed hypotheses or re-design already-run tests.

5. **Research Program Management** - A "research program" (e.g., "bio_inspired_nanochat vesicle investigation") may span 5+ sessions. Need to track progress at the program level.

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Artifact Cross-Reference Syntax

Add a `references` field to artifact items that can point to other sessions:

```json
{
  "operation": "ADD",
  "section": "hypothesis_slate",
  "payload": {
    "name": "Refined vesicle hypothesis",
    "claim": "...",
    "references": [
      { "session": "RS-20251230-bio-rrp", "item": "H2", "relation": "refines" },
      { "session": "RS-20251228-initial", "item": "T3", "relation": "informed_by" }
    ]
  }
}
```

### 2. Reference Relation Types

- `extends` - This item builds on the referenced item
- `refines` - This item is a more precise version
- `refutes` - This item contradicts the referenced item  
- `informed_by` - This item was influenced by (looser coupling)
- `supersedes` - This item replaces the referenced item
- `replicates` - This item re-tests the referenced item

### 3. Research Program Metadata

Add a top-level `research_program` field to artifacts:

```yaml
research_program:
  id: "bio-nanochat-rrp-investigation"
  name: "Bio-Inspired Nanochat RRP Investigation"
  sessions:
    - RS-20251228-initial
    - RS-20251230-bio-rrp
    - RS-20251231-followup
  current_session: RS-20251231-followup
  status: active
```

### 4. Cross-Session Search Index

Extend the artifact catalog (when built) to support queries like:
- "Find all hypotheses that reference H2 from session X"
- "Show the evolution of this hypothesis across sessions"
- "List all sessions in research program Y"

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `references` field added to delta schema (delta_output_format_v0.1.md)
- [ ] Reference relation types documented with examples
- [ ] Artifact merge validates reference syntax
- [ ] Lint warns on broken cross-session references (referenced session/item not found)
- [ ] Research program metadata schema defined
- [ ] CLI/web can query cross-session references (depends on artifact catalog)

## Dependencies

- This depends on having an artifact catalog/index (not yet beaded by others)
- Blocking: Nothing directly, but enables research program management

## Non-Goals

- Not building the full catalog search here (that is a separate bead)
- Not building a UI for visualizing hypothesis genealogy (future work)
- Not enforcing references (they remain optional annotations)

## Background: Why Sessions Are Currently Isolated

The current artifact format (artifact_schema_v0.1.md) has `metadata.session_id` but no mechanism for one artifact to reference another. Each session produces a standalone artifact. This was fine for single-session pilots but breaks down when research spans multiple sessions.

The Brenner Method emphasizes building on prior work ("stand on the shoulders of giants") - we need infrastructure that supports this at the session level.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:00:03.271284703-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T22:43:36.741230654-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-prmc","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:38:17.835895845-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-pux","title":"Test: Glossary page tests","description":"# Glossary Page Tests

## Goal
Write unit and integration tests for the /glossary page.

## Test Categories

### 1. Initial Render
- All terms from dictionary appear
- Terms are sorted alphabetically
- Category counts are correct

### 2. Search
- Typing filters visible terms
- Search matches term, short, long, analogy, why
- Clear button resets search
- "No matches" message appears when empty

### 3. Category Filtering
- Category pills filter correctly
- "All" shows everything
- Combined search + category works

### 4. Deep Linking
- Hash in URL opens corresponding details
- hashchange event triggers scroll/open
- Invalid hash is handled gracefully

### 5. Expandable Details
- Click opens details panel
- Content displays correctly (why, analogy, related)
- Related term links navigate correctly

## Files
- `apps/web/src/app/glossary/__tests__/page.test.tsx`

 {"id":"brenner_bot-q21","title":"Task: Improve mobile document navigation","description":"Make navigating within and between documents effortless on mobile.

## Purpose

With 40k+ words in the transcript, users need to navigate easily. Getting lost = abandonment.

## Current State

- Reading progress bar exists
- Table of contents exists (verify mobile experience)
- Back-to-top button exists
- Prev/Next document links at bottom

## Improvements Needed

### Table of Contents
- Accessible from header (not just inline)
- Bottom sheet presentation on mobile
- Shows current section indicator
- Tap to jump to section

### Section Headers
- Consider sticky section headers during scroll
- Or at minimum, clear visual section boundaries
- Current section always knowable

### Progress Indicator
- Verify progress bar visible but not intrusive
- Consider percentage or 'X of Y sections' text

### Quick Actions
- Easy access to TOC without scrolling up
- Floating action button or header integration

### Navigation Between Documents
- Verify prev/next cards work well on mobile
- Consider swipe gesture (separate task)

## Files to Modify

- Document viewer components
- Possibly layout.tsx for header changes
- New components if needed (e.g., mobile TOC sheet)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] TOC accessible within 1 tap from anywhere in document
- [ ] Current section always visible/knowable
- [ ] Can jump to any section in < 3 taps
- [ ] Prev/next navigation clear and usable
- [ ] No UX dead-ends","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:28:40.226724317-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:15:53.093590556-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:15:53.093590556-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented as part of brenner_bot-qka: Added MobileTocFab (floating button) that opens TOC in BottomSheet, added StickySectionIndicator showing current section number/title while scrolling with tap-to-open-TOC.  {"id":"brenner_bot-qjzw","title":"Task: Add Scorecard CLI Commands","description":"# Task: Add Scorecard CLI Commands

## Objective
CLI commands for scoring sessions, viewing feedback, and comparing scores.

## Implementation Details

### Commands

#### `brenner score <session-id>`
```bash
brenner score RS-20251230

# Output:
Session Score: RS-20251230
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Overall: 72/100 (C)

Dimension Breakdown:
  Paradox Grounding        ████████░░  15/20 (75%)
  Hypothesis Kill Rate     ██████████  20/20 (100%) ★
  Test Discriminability    ██████░░░░  10/15 (67%)
  Assumption Tracking      ████████░░  12/15 (80%)
  Third Alternative        ████░░░░░░   4/10 (40%)
  Experimental Feasibility ██████░░░░   6/10 (60%)
  Adversarial Pressure     ████░░░░░░   5/10 (50%)

Top Signals:
  ✓ At least one hypothesis killed (H-RS20251230-001)
  ✓ Kill linked to test result (T-RS20251230-002)
  ✗ "Both could be wrong" not considered
  ✗ Weak adversarial critique

Run `brenner feedback RS-20251230` for detailed suggestions.
```

#### `brenner score --all`
```bash
brenner score --all

# Output (for all sessions in current program):
Session Scores
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Session           | Score | Grade | Trend
------------------|-------|-------|-------
RS-20251230       |  72   |   C   |  -
RS-20251231       |  78   |   C   |  ↑
RS-20260101       |  85   |   B   |  ↑
RS-20260102       |  81   |   B   |  ↓

Average: 79 (C)
Trend: Improving ↑

Best: RS-20260101 (85, B)
Worst: RS-20251230 (72, C)
```

#### `brenner feedback <session-id>`
```bash
brenner feedback RS-20251230

# Output:
Feedback for RS-20251230
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Summary: Session is progressing but has 3 areas to improve.

[WARNING] Third Alternative Discovery (4/10)
  Issue: "Both could be wrong" not explicitly considered
  
  Suggestion: Explicitly ask: what if ALL current hypotheses are
  wrong? What third alternative would explain the data differently?
  
  Sydney Brenner says: "Both could be wrong." (§103)

[WARNING] Adversarial Pressure (5/10)
  Issue: Insufficient critique of hypotheses
  
  Suggestion: Actively attack your hypotheses. What assumptions
  are you making? What would a skeptic say?

[INFO] Test Discriminability (10/15)
  Issue: Some tests lack mutually exclusive predictions
  
  Suggestion: For each test, ensure hypotheses make DIFFERENT
  predictions. If H1 and H2 predict the same outcome, the test
  cannot distinguish them.
```

#### `brenner leaderboard`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Score single session with dimension breakdown
- [ ] Score all sessions with aggregation
- [ ] Feedback shows prioritized suggestions
- [ ] Leaderboard ranks sessions
- [ ] Visual progress bars for percentages
- [ ] Brenner quotes in feedback
- [ ] JSON output for scripting
